text: Perineal abdomen resection, laparoscopic due to rectal cancer. The case of a woman of fifty-nine years, without oncological or family history of importance. Time of illness of eighteen months, characterized by abdominal pain, rectorrhagia and weight loss of five kilos. According to colonoscopy, at two centimeters of anal margin, a proliferative circumferential lesion with an extension of six centimeters, friable to the biopsy. With pathological result adenocarcinoma well differentiated.

The patient is positioned in the lithotomy position for laparoscopic surgery. Four trocars are inserted: a 10mm trocar in the umbilical region, two 5mm trocars in the right hypochondriac and left flank, and a 12mm trocar in the right iliac pit. The surgeon performs a complete intra-abdominal evaluation, checking for metastasis and peritoneal implants, followed by a cavity wash and sample collection for cytological evaluation. The promontory area is identified, and dissection of the posterior peritoneum begins.

The surgeon identifies the incision plane and proceeds cephalically to locate the inferior mesenteric vessels. Two hemologs are placed proximally and a distal clip is applied to the inferior mesenteric. Dissection of the sigmoid mesocolon is performed using traction and counter-traction to release the retroperitoneum. The lateral approach to the sigmoid colon is initiated through the ethole phase for complete release.

The surgeon uses a glass sealing clip to section the sigmoid mesocolon. The intestinal section area is identified at the colosigmoideal junction, where a 60mm linear stapler is applied for sectioning. The rectum is dissected through the endopelvic fascia, completing the mesorectal dissection while preserving the genitourinary neurovascular plexus. The surgical specimen is extracted transanally via the perineal approach.
A silicone drainage tube is placed in the pelvic pit, exiting through the right contraverture. A colostomy is created in the descending colon, with the stoma exiting through the left contraverture.
